headlines.title: Supreme Court rules for marijuana user charged under same gun law as Hunter Biden
headlines.description: The Supreme Court endorsed a marijuana user's challenge to a law that bars people who consume illegal drugs from having firearms, the same law that Hunter Biden was convicted under.

headlines.title: Americans see a huge divide between haves and have-nots, new poll shows
headlines.description: A national NBC News poll finds 81% of Americans say there is more that divides than unites the wealthy and ordinary working people.

headlines.title: Trump finally sealed his Iran deal. Now talks on the ‘final’ agreement begin.
headlines.description: “This wasn’t easy.”That’s the verdict of President Donald Trump, as he finally signed a deal to end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

headlines.title: Sen. Slotkin to introduce bill to bar presidents from sending troops or armed feds to polling places
headlines.description: Sen. Elissa Slotkin, D-Mich., plans to introduce a bill Thursday that would ban the president from sending armed military or federal agents to polling sites during American elections

headlines.title: Michelle Obama speaks at the opening of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago
headlines.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama spoke at the opening of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago, praising her husband, former President Barack Obama, for his many contributions and "unflappable" composure during his time in office.

headlines.title: Vance discusses differences between Iran deal and nuclear agreement Obama negotiated
headlines.description: During a White House press briefing, Vice President JD Vance compared how the memorandum of understanding the Trump administration has with Iran differs from the nuclear agreement previously negotiated during the Obama administration.

headlines.title: Barack Obama’s first interview on Meet the Press (2004) | NBC News Archives
headlines.description: JULY 25, 2004 — Illinois State Sen. Barack Obama (D) joined Meet the Press for the first time after being selected to deliver the keynote address at the 2004 Democratic National Convention — a speech that would help launch him onto the national political stage and change the trajectory of his career.

headlines.title: U.S. deportation tracker: Counting arrests, detentions and border crossings
headlines.description: President Donald Trump came into office promising the largest mass deportation in U.S history, targeting the more than 10 million unauthorized migrants living in the United States.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: California billionaire tax proposal qualifies for the November ballot
headlines.description: A proposal to raise taxes on the wealthiest Californians has qualified for the November ballot, state officials said Wednesday, setting up what could be an expensive and divisive fight

headlines.title: Challenger concedes to democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George in D.C. mayoral primary
headlines.description: The leading challenger to Washington, D.C., City Councilmember Janeese Lewis George, a democratic socialist, conceded the Democratic primary for mayor Thursday, putting her in line to lead the capital city and manage its relationship with President Donald Trump

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.
